perturbation.original_text: “Purpose”: means the exchange of Confidential Information, trade secrets, know -how and samples in order to evaluate and pursue the objective of one or more potential business arrangements with respect to Kenway’s potential to supply the Company with information, products and/or designs that are proprietary to the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: “Purpose”: means the exchange of Confidential Information, trade secrets, know -how and samples in order to evaluate and pursue the objective of one or more potential business arrangements with respect to Kenway’s potential to steal information, products and/or designs that are proprietary to the Company.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'supply' to 'steal' in the definition of 'Purpose' creates a severe contradiction. The rest of the agreement assumes a legitimate and collaborative purpose for the exchange of confidential information. For instance, Section 5.1 states that 'Each Disclosing Party warrants that it has the right to disclose its Confidential Information to the Recipient and to authorize the Recipient to use such Confidential Information for the Purpose.' This warranty is directly contradicted by the altered definition of purpose, which now implies Kenway intends to 'steal' the Company's proprietary information. This creates significant uncertainty as to the enforceability of the agreement, since the stated 'purpose' is now illegal and unethical.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.1 Each Disclosing Party warrants that it has the right to disclose its Confidential Information to the Recipient and to authorize the Recipient to use such Confidential Information for the Purpose.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'supply' with 'steal' in the definition of 'Purpose'. This directly contradicts section 5.1, which warrants that each party has the right to disclose information and authorize its use 'for the Purpose'. Since the 'Purpose' is now defined as stealing, the warranty in 5.1 is undermined, as it's unlikely a party can warrant the right to steal information. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because the changed definition now implies an illegal purpose, conflicting with the warranty that assumes a legitimate purpose.

perturbation.original_text: 2.1 The Recipient shall keep the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information confidential and, except with the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party, shall: (a) not use or exploit the Confidential Information in any way except for the P urpose;
perturbation.changed_text: 2.1 The Recipient shall keep the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information confidential and, shall: (a) use or exploit the Confidential Information in any way except for the P urpose;
perturbation.explanation: Removing the phrase 'except with the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party, shall not' and adding shall creates a direct in-text contradiction within Section 2.1 itself. The first part of the sentence states the Recipient must keep the information confidential. However, part (a) of the obligation now states that the Recipient *shall* 'use or exploit the Confidential Information'. This creates complete uncertainty in enforcement. Is the recipient required to keep the information confidential, or are they explicitly permitted to use and exploit it?
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1 The Recipient shall keep the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information confidential and, shall: (a) use or exploit the Confidential Information in any way except for the P urpose; (b) not disclose or make available the Confidential Information in whole or in part to any third party, except as expressly permitted by this Agreement; (c) not copy, reduce to writing or otherwise record the Confidential Information except as strictly n ecessary for the Purpose (and any such copies, reductions to writing and records shall be the property of the Disclosing Party); (d) keep separate the Confidential Information from all documents and other records of the Recipient; (e) apply the same security meas ures and degree of care to the Confidential Information as the Recipient applies to its own confidential information, which the Recipient warrants as providing adequate protection from unauthori zed disclosure, copying or use; and (f) keep a written record of: any document or other Confidential Information received from the other in tangible form; any copy made of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts itself and therefore also the contradicted text (which is identical). The original text says the recipient 'shall not' use the confidential information. The changed text states that the recipient 'shall' use the confidential information. This is a direct contradiction. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, thus the contradiction is present in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: 5.  Warranty and Indemnity 5.1 Each Disclosing Party warrants that it has the right to disclose its Confidential Information to the Recipient and to authorize the Recipient to use such Confidential Information for the Purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Warranty and Indemnity 5.1 Each Disclosing Party warrants that it does not have the right to disclose its Confidential Information to the Recipient and does not authorize the Recipient to use such Confidential Information for the Purpose.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'has the right to disclose' to 'does not have the right to disclose' and 'authorize the Recipient to use' to 'does not authorize the Recipient to use' creates a direct contradiction of the core purpose of the agreement. This conflicts with section 2.1, which requires the Recipient to maintain confidentiality while using the information for the agreed-upon Purpose. The modified section 5.1 invalidates the legal basis for the transfer and use of confidential information. If the Disclosing Party warrants that it *cannot* authorize the use of its confidential information, the agreement becomes unenforceable and the Recipient has no legitimate right to use the information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1 The Recipient shall keep the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information confidential and, except with the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party, shall: (a) not use or exploit the Confidential Information in any way except for the P urpose; (b) not disclose or make available the Confidential Information in whole or in part to any third party, except as expressly permitted by this Agreement; (c) not copy, reduce to writing or otherwise record the Confidential Information except as strictly n ecessary for the Purpose (and any such copies, reductions to writing and records shall be the property of the Disclosing Party); (d) keep separate the Confidential Information from all documents and other records of the Recipient; (e) apply the same security meas ures and degree of care to the Confidential Information as the Recipient applies to its own confidential information, which the Recipient warrants as providing adequate protection from unauthori zed disclosure, copying or use; and (f) keep a written record of: any document or other Confidential Information received from the other in tangible form; any copy made of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 5.1 directly contradicts Section 2.1. Section 5.1 now states that the Disclosing Party 'does not have the right to disclose' and 'does not authorize' the use of Confidential Information, while Section 2.1 obligates the Recipient to keep the Confidential Information confidential and only use it for the Purpose. This creates a direct conflict as the recipient cannot fulfill their obligation in 2.1 if the disclosing party does not authorize them to have/use the information as described in the modified 5.1. This is an example of a Structural Flaw that creates an In-Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 11.  Notice s 11.1 Any notice or other communication required to be given under this Agreement, shall be in writing and shall be delivered personally, or sent by pre -paid first class post or recorded delivery or by commercial courier, to each Party required to receive the notice or communication at its address as set out below:
perturbation.changed_text: 11. Notice s 11.1 Any notice or other communication required to be given under this Agreement, shall be in writing and shall be delivered personally, or sent by pre -paid first class post or recorded delivery or by commercial courier, to each Party required to receive the notice or communication at its address as set out below: 11.3 A notice or other communication required to be given under this Agreement shall be validly given if sent by e-mail.
perturbation.explanation: By adding '11.3 A notice or other communication required to be given under this Agreement shall be validly given if sent by e-mail.' to Section 11.1, it introduces an in-text contradiction to 11.3 which says that A notice or other communication required to be given under this Agreement shall not be v alidly given if sent by e- mail.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11.3 A notice or other communication required to be given under this Agreement shall not be v alidly given if sent by e- mail.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds '11.3 A notice or other communication required to be given under this Agreement shall be validly given if sent by e-mail.' to section 11.1, which directly contradicts section 11.3 which states 'A notice or other communication required to be given under this Agreement shall not be v alidly given if sent by e- mail.' This introduces a strong in-text contradiction regarding whether email is a valid form of communication under the agreement.
